audit_items.audit_check: Method scope draft preserves safe abstract toy-only and real-bio exclusion wording.
audit_items.boundary_note: The method-scope draft excludes real-biological operation.
audit_items.expected: synthetic toy-only scope and real-bio exclusions
audit_items.observed: This work presents an internally audited, safety-bounded, safe abstract toy-only governance pipeline for organizing manuscript patch decisions, claim boundaries, safe claim language, and planned placement of that language. The central contribution is a controlled governance layer for safe abstract toy manuscript development: it records decision lineage, separates allowed from deferred and prohibited claims, and packages audited wording before any manuscript modification is authorized. All artifacts in this lineage are synthetic, abstract, unitless, non-operational, and toy-only. The lineage does not import real biological datasets, use real receptor parameters, simulate real pathogens, or support operational biological targeting. The staged lineage proceeds through proposal, dry-run, structural gate, plan, checklist, audit, decision ledger, finding register, consistency audit, claim boundary registration, claim language packaging, claim language audit, placement planning, and placement-plan audit. The internal safety counters preserve zero application permission, zero checklist execution, zero applied patches, zero manuscript mutation, zero readiness approval, zero external validation, zero proof assistant verification, zero new citation addition, and zero real-biological operational capability. The claim governance layer classifies manuscript wording into currently allowed, deferred, and prohibited families, preventing governance descriptions from drifting into readiness, validation, or real-biological operational claims. The current evidence base does not establish external validation, independent experimental support, formal proof, citation-completed claims, applied manuscript patches, or manuscript submission readiness. The framework does not model real pathogens, real receptors, operational host targeting, infectivity optimization, immune evasion, host-range prediction, wet-lab protocols, or actionable biosafety-risk procedures. Future work may only introduce stronger claims after separately audited milestones for safe manuscript patch application, citation integration, non-operational toy evaluation, proof pathway, or submission readiness review.

audit_items.audit_check: Blocked expansion and usage-boundary language prevent overclaim drift.

audit_items.observed: Abstract bounded contribution sentence This work presents an internally audited, safety-bounded, safe abstract toy-only governance pipeline for organizing manuscript patch decisions, claim boundaries, safe claim language, and planned placement of that language. Use only as a governance contribution statement. Do not add validation, performance, biological realism, or submission readiness. Introduction problem and contribution framing The central contribution is a controlled governance layer for safe abstract toy manuscript development: it records decision lineage, separates allowed from deferred and prohibited claims, and packages audited wording before any manuscript modification is authorized. Use for framing the contribution without implying empirical success. Do not claim the framework has been scientifically validated. Method Scope scope limiter All artifacts in this lineage are synthetic, abstract, unitless, non-operational, and toy-only. The lineage does not import real biological datasets, use real receptor parameters, simulate real pathogens, or support operational biological targeting. Use to define the safe abstract toy-only boundary. Do not add real biological examples, parameters, datasets, or host-targeting language. Pipeline Overview artifact lineage description The staged lineage proceeds through proposal, dry-run, structural gate, plan, checklist, audit, decision ledger, finding register, consistency audit, claim boundary registration, claim language packaging, claim language audit, placement planning, and placement-plan audit. Use as artifact history only. Do not state that manuscript patches have been applied. Safety Controls zero-counter safety statement The internal safety counters preserve zero application permission, zero checklist execution, zero applied patches, zero manuscript mutation, zero readiness approval, zero external validation, zero proof assistant verification, zero new citation addition, and zero real-biological operational capability. Use as a boundary-control statement, not as scientific evidence. Do not treat zero counters as validation or proof. Claim Governance allowed/deferred/prohibited claim separation The claim governance layer classifies manuscript wording into currently allowed, deferred, and prohibited families, preventing governance descriptions from drifting into readiness, validation, or real-biological operational claims. Use to explain claim discipline. Do not present claim classification as approval to submit. Limitations evidence boundary The current evidence base does not establish external validation, independent experimental support, formal proof, citation-completed claims, applied manuscript patches, or manuscript submission readiness. Use unchanged until future audited evidence exists. Do not weaken this limitation without a future official evidence-upgrade milestone. Safety Exclusions real-bio non-claim The framework does not model real pathogens, real receptors, operational host targeting, infectivity optimization, immune evasion, host-range prediction, wet-lab protocols, or actionable biosafety-risk procedures. Use as explicit exclusion language. Do not add operational biological mechanisms or procedures. Future Work conditional evidence-upgrade pathway Future work may only introduce stronger claims after separately audited milestones for safe manuscript patch application, citation integration, non-operational toy evaluation, proof pathway, or submission readiness review. Use to describe conditional future work only. Do not imply that future milestones have already occurred.
